blocks.content: While her friends were chatting, Shirone squinted and observed carefully.
The atmosphere felt oddly familiar.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Wait… is that Ms. Siena?

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Ms. Siena?
Neid leaned forward and took a closer look.
It was definitely her.
She wasn't wearing glasses, and her trademark neatly tied-up hair was loose, but it was unmistakably Siena.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Wow. That's incredible. Can a person change that much?

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Right? With makeup, she looks like a completely different person. By the way, Shirone, how did you recognize her? You've got a sharp eye for women, huh?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Huh? No. She just looked like her.
The reason Shirone recognized her at a glance was that he had seen Siena with her hair down before.
But he couldn't bring himself to mention that the teacher had visited him late at night, so he brushed it off.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: But where is she going? She's even wearing makeup.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Today's her day off, and it's a holiday too. Maybe she's on a date or something.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: But wasn't Ms. Siena distant from men?

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Still, she must have someone she meets. At 26, she's at the age where people start thinking about marriage. Even teachers have to get married someday.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Hmm. I guess so…
Neid stroked his chin, lost in thought, when a sudden idea lit up in his mind.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Should we follow her? I'm curious.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Follow her? Why? It's her private life.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: We don't have anything else to do anyway. Sitting here won't solve anything, and my head feels like it's about to explode. It's a holiday, so as long as we're back before dark, it's fine.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Do you even know how to tail someone without getting caught? She's a certified 6th-rank mage. Even without Spirit Zone, her awareness is on another level. We'll be spotted immediately.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Then let's do this. Iruki, you track the teacher, and Shirone will act as the relay. I'll stop by the lab and come back.
Shirone saw through Neid's plan.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: The invisibility cloak?

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Exactly! If we follow her under the invisibility cloak, even the teacher won't notice. She has no reason to deploy Spirit Zone while on a date. What do you think?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: That's not a bad idea. Alright, I'll start tailing her now. Shirone, you bring Neid to me later.
As Iruki sprang up from the bench and began chasing Siena, Shirone sighed.
These kids were truly exceptional when it came to mischief.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Then I'll go get the invisibility cloak. Shirone, you bring Neid to me later.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Then I'll go get the invisibility cloak. Shirone, don't lose track of Iruki. Can you keep checking points while moving?
Since the city's layout was constrained by artificial structures like roads, tracking key points would give them a rough idea of her route.
They couldn't let Neid fall too far behind, so it was crucial to determine the rendezvous point midway.
Reluctantly, Shirone nodded.
He hated how his mind had already calculated everything even in this situation.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Got it. But hurry. I can't guarantee more than 10 minutes.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Haha! That's our Shirone! I'm counting on you!
Neid dashed off to the research lab, while Shirone tailed Iruki and returned after 10 minutes to pick up Neid at the main gate.
From there, they ran quickly and caught up with Iruki in another 10 minutes.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: This is weird.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Why? Did you lose the teacher?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: No. But I almost did. She's unusually alert, as if she's on a battlefield.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Oho. This is getting interesting. Maybe she's dealing with forbidden items for extra cash, just like us?
Shirone shot Neid a disapproving look.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The teacher wouldn't do something like that.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Anyway, let's go. Now that we have the invisibility cloak, we can get closer.
The three covered themselves with the cloak and continued following Siena.
As they entered the commoners' district, the streets were bustling with people setting up shop since dawn, making the tailing easier.
Siena stopped at a two-story house.
The trio peeked out from under the cloak, watching to see who would open the door.
When Siena glanced around, they ducked back in like turtles, their hearts pounding.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Why is she so cautious? Whose house is this? It's just an ordinary commoner's home.
Driven by curiosity, they peeked out again.
There was no need to freeze like criminals when they hadn't done anything wrong.
A young man opened the door and stepped out.
The moment he saw Siena, they embraced deeply.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: W-what in the world…!
Shirone and his friends were shocked.
Siena's nickname, 'Frozen Siena,' referred not only to her magical specialty but also to her cold demeanor toward men.
Yet here she was, secretly involved with one.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: What's going on? That was definitely a man, right?

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: We'll find out if we go in.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What? How can we just enter someone's house?

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: But we can't leave now, right? We need to know for sure.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: The teacher is single, so of course she can meet men. Why are you making such a fuss?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Something smells fishy. There's nothing wrong with the teacher meeting a man. But why hide it? She's officially single at school. And her being so cautious is also suspicious.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: That's exactly it. So let's check it out.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What are you planning?

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: We'll go in through the basement storage. Don't worry. Even if we get caught, it's not a big deal. It's not like it's a stranger's house since the teacher went in.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Isn't that… a very subjective way of thinking?

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: I don't know. Anyway, I'm going. I'm dying of curiosity.
As Neid and Iruki moved, Shirone sighed and followed.
He didn't like going along with it, but leaving the two alone was riskier.
At the basement entrance, Neid examined the iron door.
It was horizontally installed and firmly locked.
He pulled out a pen-like device from his pocket and pressed a button, causing a sharp metal needle to extend.
It was one of Neid's patented inventions: a lock-picking tool.
Picking a lock required precise pressure on its internal mechanisms, and Neid's device could adjust the needle to a hundredth of a centimeter, making common locks easy to crack.
After fiddling with the lock, Neid twisted his wrist, and with a click, it opened.
Inside, a ladder led downward.
Shirone felt a sense of futility.
People trusted locks to keep them safe, forgetting that locks were also man-made.
The scent of onions, typical of a food storage, tickled their noses as they descended.
While Shirone closed the iron door, Neid pressed his ear against the door leading upstairs to listen for movement.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Good. The first floor is quiet too. Let's go up.
Under the invisibility cloak, the trio floated like ghosts with only their heads visible.
The first floor had an open-plan living room and kitchen, but no one was there.
Ascending to the second floor, they sensed movement from a room at the end of the hallway.
Siena's voice could be heard.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: What are you doing? Hurry up.

blocks.speaker: A man
blocks.content: Wait. I'm not ready yet. Take off your clothes first.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: When will your wife be back?

blocks.speaker: A man
blocks.content: She went to the market. It'll take at least two hours.
Neid couldn't contain his excitement—or rather, his shock.
Shirone felt similarly.
He never imagined the man Siena was meeting was married.

blocks.speaker: A man
blocks.content: Aren't you wearing it today?

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: No. Straighten your back a bit more.

blocks.speaker: A man
blocks.content: Like this?

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Yeah, like that. Lift your legs slightly. Yes, that's it.
The trio's faces turned bright red.
As adults, they understood romantic relationships, but this was an affair.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Shall we begin? Don't move.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Don't worry. I've held out so far.
Neid looked at his friends with anger.
Understanding his intent, Shirone and Iruki nodded.
Love knew no borders, but considering the betrayed wife's feelings, they had to stop this.
Neid's hand trembled as he gripped the doorknob.
Waiting longer would provide irrefutable evidence, but he couldn't bear to see their respected teacher in such a state.
The doorknob turned roughly, and the three barged in without hesitation.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: You scoundrel! Ms. Siena! I'm so disappointed!
Neid's shout was met with silence.
Shirone and Iruki, who had braced themselves to close their eyes, stared blankly at the scene inside the room.
It was nothing like what they had imagined.
Siena, sitting on the bed, turned toward the door.
Across from her was a large canvas.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Huh? What?
The trio gaped like fish.
A single thought crossed their minds: this was a massive misunderstanding, a distortion of reality.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: What? How did you all get here…?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Huh? No, it's just… we were sitting in the park, zoning out, when you walked by. So we…

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: So you did follow me!

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Eek! We're sorry!
The three shrank back simultaneously.
Given her personality, a scolding wouldn't be the end of it.
But contrary to their expectations, no further reprimand came.
Siena seemed lost in thought.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: How did they manage to follow me?
She had been vigilant, avoiding Spirit Zone but maintaining awareness.
Yet her students had tailed her effortlessly.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: What's that behind you? Hand it over.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Neid. Is this another one of your patented inventions?

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Yes.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: You really made this yourself?

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: B-but I didn't sell it on the black market! I have some conscience left.
Siena snapped.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Sigh.
At Siena's sigh, the trio flinched.
But she didn't lecture them further.
As a certified 6th-rank mage, being tailed was more a reflection of her own carelessness than her students' mischief.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: At first, we followed you as a joke, but when we saw you hugging a man, we came in. After eavesdropping, we thought you were having an affair and…

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: What? An affair?
Siena's face turned as red as a beet.
It was the first time they'd seen her so flustered.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: An affair? How could you think that of me!

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: But it sounded like that! How could we not misunderstand? Telling you to take off your clothes, adjust your posture… Who is that man anyway?

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: He must be amazing. But if it's okay to ask, what happened to his eyes…?
Siena's expression darkened, making Neid regret his question.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: He lost his eyes saving me.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: What? Because of you?
When I was nine, I fell into Overflow.
I began vaguely understanding the world's principles, and everything terrified me.
I cried every day.
Even now, I remember it as sheer torment.
Armin oppa saved me.
He pulled me from darkness into light.
He was only 11 at the time.
Eleven was still a child.
Yet he rescued Siena from Overflow, a testament to his extraordinary talent.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: So his advice helped you overcome Overflow.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: No. I wasn't that strong.
I was just a brat who acted high and mighty because of the Olipher name.
Back then, I hated oppa.
He always told me the same thing: 'Never look away from what comes to you.'
It annoyed me.
If something's scary, you close your eyes.
If you can't bear it, you run.
Looking back, I was such a fool.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Then how did you overcome Overflow?
I hated oppa finding me, so I'd hide in the mountains, fields, or riversides.
He always found me, but I'd run again.
One day, during this game of hide-and-seek… the incident happened.
It was the rainy season, and it had poured for days.
Even in that weather, I wandered by the river.
Looking back, it might've been fine if I'd just passed by.
But coincidentally, the dam upstream collapsed.
Before I knew it, I was swept away by the flood.
It was massive enough to submerge half the village.
Siena recalled the events of 17 years ago.
She was swallowed by the river without even screaming.
When she surfaced, thrashing desperately, the scenery was completely different.
Debris and livestock floated downstream, while broken trees drifted ominously.
For the first time, Siena tasted the terror of death.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Help me. Please…!
She knew no one would come.
This area, far from the school, was a no-man's-land between a stream and a swamp.
Now a raging river, no one would expect a little girl to be here.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Help! Someone, help me!
For the first time, Siena begged shamelessly.
Her pride as the Olipher family's only daughter meant nothing against nature's fury.
Her innate talent couldn't overcome the flood.
All she could do was scream for help.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Siena!
Like a divine revelation, Siena mustered all her strength to look up.
Armin was perched precariously on a bent tree branch over the river.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Oppa! Oppa!

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Give me your hand!
Just as Siena tried to grab it, a sudden whirlpool dragged her under.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Siena!
When she resurfaced, it was her legs, not her face, that broke the surface.
Without hesitation, Armin dove in.
Riding the current, he swam swiftly, grabbed Siena, and pulled her head above water.
Choking, Siena was barely conscious.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Gah! Gah!

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Snap out of it! We need to get out!

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: S-save me! Save me!
Past the whirlpools, even deadlier obstacles awaited.
Armin expanded his Spirit Zone to navigate around them.
At 11, it was his only option.
